decision: Is it worth continuing to develop the product, and specifically to keep investing in "Tasks 2.0", or to stop

files.name: decision-brief.md
files.description: Brief from the VP that frames the decision question without answering it

files.name: usage-analytics.csv
files.description: Monthly usage table: DAU/MAU, "Tasks 2.0" adoption, and weekly use that peaks and then declines

files.name: churn-cohort.md
files.description: Messy churn data by cohort; churn climbs, and the people who tried the feature and were disappointed churn the most

files.name: customer-survey.md
files.description: Survey results: NPS declining, "Tasks 2.0" as the number one complaint, and demand for simplicity and the customer portal

files.name: competitor-moves.md
files.description: Moves by Monday and Notion and cheap tools entering the freelancer's territory

files.name: market-trend-note.md
files.description: Trend note: the freelancer market is growing, but the direction is simplicity and real time savings, not more complexity

files.name: revenue-pricing-snapshot.md
files.description: Revenue and cost picture: MRR growth almost flat, and "Tasks 2.0" is a cost center with no direct revenue

files.name: tasks2-original-pitch.md
files.description: The original marketing announcement of "Tasks 2.0", for reference against the gap between the promise and reality

files.name: office-lease-memo.md
files.description: Memo about the office lease renewal, unrelated to the product decision

files.name: vendor-invoice-coffee.md
files.description: Vendor invoice for office coffee supply, pure noise

files.name: recruiting-email.md
files.description: Recruiting email about a developer interview round, not relevant to the decision

files.description: The ready prompts the learner copies: the Point stage and the POWER stage
